title: Scrapy Architecture, Scheduler, Middleware, AutoThrottle, and Observability · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent site owner provides a different origin, URL space, markup fault, indexing policy, accessibility need, resource budget, dynamic boundary, and failure condition and transfers scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, autothrottle, and observability into a changed Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners prog…
objectives: Trace engine, scheduler, downloader, spider, item pipeline, signals, extensions, requests, responses, failures, and ownership in Scrapy.
Map canonical request identity, priorities, persistence, depth, retries, duplicate filtering, and resume semantics into scheduler evidence.
Reason about request and response traversal order, built-in middleware, exceptions, short-circuiting, and settings merge before customization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Scrapy Component Flow
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab site-quality team handles case 9242 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Crawl Observability
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-crawl-observability-1hf2ix" that uses case 4221 to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Crawl Observability.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Crawl Observability into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Crawl Observability, keep Crawl Observability connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-crawl-observability-1hf2ix
# Competency: scraper-crawl-observability.
# Case 4221: a clean package and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_crawl_observability_1hf2ix(component_boundaries: int = 8, verified_boundaries: int = 8, middleware_orders: int = 6, metric_families: int = 10, hidden_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1hf2ix = "1hf2ix-xi2fh1-4221"
    assert evidence_variant_1hf2ix
    evidence_axes_1hf2ix = (
        "authorization1hf2ix",
        "url4221",
        "responsexi2fh1",
        "tree1hf2ix4221",
        "frontier42211hf2ix",
        "reportxi2fh14221",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1hf2ix)) == 6
    if component_boundaries < 6 or verified_boundaries < 0:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "scrapy-architecture"
    if verified_boundaries != component_boundaries:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "component-reconciliation"
    if middleware_orders < 4 or metric_families < 8:
        return False, metric_families, "scale-evidence"
    if hidden_failures > 0:
        return False, hidden_failures, "observability-gap"
    assert verified_boundaries == component_boundaries and hidden_failures == 0
    return True, metric_families, "observable-scrapy-transfer"
